title: Route Snapshot Card
prompt: Create a production-ready Route Snapshot Card using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original project card presents a delivery route as three readable checkpoints and lets the user expand a concise checkpoint snapshot. Use a pale gray Bento stage, a white 22px-radius card, #111111 primary text, #4A4A4F metadata, a #ECECEE border, and only a violet route marker plus a cyan completion marker. The card itself must never use a gradient. Show a title, percentage, three small route stops, and an explicit View checkpoint button. Clicking it, Enter, or Space expands an inline snapshot with the active location, time, and a short action. Use local state, semantic button elements, and aria-expanded with aria-controls. Animate the snapshot with opacity and y transform over 220ms cubic-bezier(.22,1,.36,1), and move the selected marker with a spring around stiffness 320 and damping 26. Keep the resting server-rendered state informative, do not use remote imagery, random values, browser storage, network requests, or uncleaned timers. The layout must shrink gracefully at 320px, use a 44px target, preserve logical tab order, and provide a dark visible focus outline. Ensure all labels meet AA contrast and do not depend on color alone: use text and a current marker. In prefers-reduced-motion, replace movement with a brief opacity transition and leave the opened snapshot stable. Return complete paste-ready client code and a pure CSS approximation with suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML: <article class="route-snapshot-card"><button aria-expanded="false">View checkpoint</button></article> */
.route-snapshot-card{max-width:22rem;padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff;color:#111;box-shadow:0 7px 22px rgba(17,17,17,.06)}.route-snapshot-card button{min-height:44px;padding:0 16px;border:1px solid #111;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;font-weight:800}.route-snapshot-card button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.route-snapshot-card *{transition:none!important}}
